name: My lawyer used AI for my case docs—can the other side get those records?
description: Lawyer-directed AI might be protected as work product; client-only use isn't.[1]

mainEntity.name: My lawyer used AI for my case docs—can the other side get those records?

acceptedAnswer.text: Lawyer-directed AI might be protected as work product; client-only use isn't.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Possibly not, if the lawyer directed the AI use as part of representation, it might qualify for work product protection.[1] Courts have noted this narrow exception where AI acts as the lawyer's agent, like an investigator.[1] Key factors: Attorney must affirmatively direct use; independent client AI chats don't qualify; verify accuracy to avoid hallucinations.[1][3] Tell your lawyer about any personal AI use; they can assess protection and certify filings. Courts increasingly require AI disclosure in some filings.[3]
